# `energy/state_coal_consumption_mt`

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by `python -m yen_gov indicator-pages`. Do not hand-edit. -->

**Title**: State coal consumption (Mt, FY06–FY25)  
**One-line**: Per-state domestic coal consumption in million tonnes per fiscal year, summed across all coal grades produced or imported within the state (raw coal + washed coal + middlings + lignite).  
**Source artifact last modified**: 2026-05-16 (auto-generated)  
**Source artifact**: [`datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_coal_consumption_mt.json`](../../../../datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_coal_consumption_mt.json)

## Definition

Per-state domestic coal consumption in million tonnes per fiscal year, summed across all coal grades produced or imported within the state (raw coal + washed coal + middlings + lignite). Coal is by far India's largest primary-energy source: states with high coal consumption are typically those that host large thermal generation fleets (Maharashtra, UP, MP, Chhattisgarh) or heavy industry (steel, cement).

## Coverage

- **Temporal**: 2005-04..2024-04 (20 periods)
- **Spatial**: India (states + UTs, coal-consuming states only) (26 entities)
- **Rows on disk**: 450

## Methodology vintage

NITI Aayog ICED ``/energy/fuel-sources/coal/consumption-domestic-state`` (Coal Controller's Office / Ministry of Coal upstream). Aggregated by SUM of the 4 component grades (raw, washed, middlings, lignite); the precomputed ``TOTAL COAL`` rows are dropped to avoid double-counting (they exist for only the most-recent FYs).

## Notes

Coal consumption is a *consumption* statistic — the state where coal is burned, not the state where it is mined. For the production side, see installed-capacity-by-source (coal facet) and the generation-by-source artifact.
